data_notes.roas: REAL as of 2026-07-11 (previously null — store had no LTV data). roas_standard/roas_conservative computed from facility_day's MSR-native LTV/leads/conversion columns + real ad_spend, same formula as nectar/roas.py (validated: matches the already-confirmed live-pull June ROAS figures exactly). Reflects 2026-06 (the latest month with real ad_spend) for 23 ad-running facilities; the other 6 run no ads and correctly show roas_standard=null, not a fabricated 0.
data_notes.revenue_yoy: FIXED 2026-07-18 (inflated-YoY bug): current vs. the SAME CALENDAR DATE last year, SAME-STORE ONLY, EXACT-DATE match on both sides (portfolio_query.revenue_yoy() — no 'nearest prior snapshot' fallback). Previously the 2026-07-11 one-time backfill froze AFAM/NSS/SL Reno's prior-year side at 2025-07-11 while the current side kept growing daily, silently comparing an 11-day MTD to a 17+-day MTD and inflating YoY (AFAM read +39.5% vs a true roughly-flat figure). Every export now self-heals: it live-pulls the exact prior-year same-date MSR (nectar_ingest.ingest_date) for any facility missing it before computing YoY, so the comparison can no longer silently drift out of alignment. FIXED 2026-07-19 (flat-KPI bug + partial-day pin): the current side is taken at mtd_as_of = the freshest COMPLETE MTD snapshot on/before the export date (latest facility_day date with revenue_mtd_msr, EXCLUDING the in-progress current day), not the raw export date — so (a) when the export runs ahead of / without today's ingest the exact-date match no longer finds an empty current side and collapses every scope's delta to null (which the site rendered as a misleading 'flat vs / $0' Revenue-vs-LY KPI), and (b) an intraday snapshot never pits a partial current day against a complete prior-year day. prior_date is that same effective date's own same-date-prior-year, so current and prior stay calendar-aligned. Same source and same-store method as the MTD PDF's KPI strip (net_revenue.revenue_mtd), so the values match. This export's mtd_as_of = 2026-07-19, prior = 2025-07-19. All 29 facilities had an exact same-date match on both sides — none excluded.
data_notes.revenue_mtd_accuracy: FIXED 2026-07-11: revenue_authoritative now reads net_revenue.revenue_mtd verbatim off each facility's latest MSR snapshot (facility_day.revenue_mtd_msr), replacing the SUM(revenue_net)-over-days proxy that overstated MTD by ~15-27% (see README.md).
data_notes.net_move_ins_mtd: RECONCILED 2026-07-19 (Travis-approved): move_activity_mtd.{move_ins,move_outs,net_move_ins} and facilities[].{move_ins_mtd,move_outs_mtd,net_move_ins_mtd} now read Nectar's OWN MTD counters (facility_day.move_ins_mtd_msr / move_outs_mtd_msr, written verbatim off rental_metrics.*.mtd by nectar_ingest) via portfolio_query.move_activity_mtd_msr() — the SAME source the weekly MTD PDF's KPI strip uses — instead of the daily-sum of the move_ins/move_outs columns, which ran a few units low (portfolio 16 vs the PDF's 20). Any facility whose MSR-native column isn't populated yet falls back to its daily sum, so the total is never silently undercounted.
data_notes.leads_by_channel_mtd: real per-channel counts, but facility_day_leadsource only started 2026-07-09 — as of 2026-07-20 it covers a partial window, not the full MTD leads_total figure; fills out day by day. (Unrelated to this pass's fixes — still gapped.)
data_notes.ad_spend_month: reflects 2026-06, the latest CLOSED month — ad spend refreshes monthly at month-close, not on this twice-daily cadence. roas above uses the SAME month so spend and leads/LTV are period-coherent.
data_notes.rent_per_sqft: FIXED 2026-07-11: now reads space_metrics.average_rent_area.occupied verbatim (Travis's "economic occupancy $/sqft") off each facility's latest MSR snapshot — captured on every ingest, no derived/summed proxy.
data_notes.units_total_and_net_move_ins_ytd: ADDED 2026-07-11 for the facility map hover cards. facilities[].units_total is the latest facility_day snapshot (store-native, no extra API call). facilities[].net_move_ins_ytd is read verbatim from a live MSR pull's own rental_metrics.net_count_movein_moveout.ytd field (same reasoning as nectar/sil1_daily_card.py) because facility_day's daily move_ins/move_outs only cover 2026-07-01 onward, too short a window to sum a full-year YTD locally. 29 facility(ies) failed the live pull and show net_move_ins_ytd=null (not fabricated): DEM1, DOV1, ELP5, ELP6, ELP7, ELP8, ELP9, GRN1, SIL1, RNO1, 301, 304, 305, 306, 309, 311, 314, 316, 319, 323, 325, 326, 328, 329, 333, 338, 339, 341, 343.
data_notes.delinquency_aging: RECONCILED 2026-07-19 to the NSS Delinquency Report (Travis-approved): now sourced from delinquent_leases (per-lease days_late/$) via delinquency_query.site_aging(), NOT the old facility_day Hummingbird UNIT buckets. The site and the report now read the SAME 90+ auction tail (beyond_90_units = leases with days_late >= 90, = delinquency_query.auction_tail(90)) and the SAME $ pool. 'units' = delinquent LEASES, 'amount' = SUM(balance_owed). Bucket edges (31-60 ... 361+) are unchanged so the funnel JSON contract/render is unchanged; only the source table + grain moved (units->leases, facility_day->delinquent_leases). delinquency_pct_dollars (delinquent $ / monthly rent roll) is the report's headline rate; delinquency_pct_units (delinquent leases / occupied units) is the secondary. Both use the live rent roll (rent_roll field) from the same /leases/ pull the report uses; null (not fabricated) if that pull is unavailable. facilities[].delinquency_pct is now the $-based per-facility rate (was units-based facility_day).
data_notes.storelocal_protection: ADDED 2026-07-17 for the site's Storelocal Protection section. Same stats — and the same MSR fields — as the Portfolio Snapshot's SLP page (nectar/slp.py): insured units (insurance.total), collected premiums (deposits_by_product_type.total_coverage, MSR-native MTD), recognized insurance revenue (revenue.insurance MTD), product-type split. Penetration is SUM(insured)/SUM(occupied) at query time (portfolio_query.protection()), the MSR's own headline definition; a total-units variant is also exported. coverage_in_force_est is an ESTIMATE floor (insured x $2,500 base tier — $3.5K/$5K plans push it higher; no per-tenant tier exists in the MSR). SLP columns only exist in facility_day from 2026-07-16 onward (that day's rows were re-ingested live when the columns landed; no earlier raw MSR payloads were persisted to backfill from).
data_notes.revenue_history_backfill: 13 months of MSR history (2025-05 through 2026-06, monthly grain) are now in facility_day, reused from nectar.history's existing snapshots/history_<key>.jsonl cache (zero new API calls for 12 of those months) plus one live pull each for 2026-06-30 and last year's same calendar date. Historical LTV was NOT backfilled beyond the current month + 2026-06 — a ROAS trend chart would need that; revenue/occupancy trend is ready for a future date-range selector today.
